title: satureja microphylla subsp. rodriguezii
intro: Satureja microphylla subsp. rodriguezii, also known as Rodriguez's savory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Andean highlands in South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Traditionally, Rodriguez's savory is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
